title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Reasoning: Choosing COORDINATE yields 100 utility only if all 100 other civilizations also choose it, but with random independent decisions, the probability of this is vanishingly small (e.g., near zero for even modest randomness), making expected utility effectively 0. ISOLATE guarantees at least 20 utility if all isolate, but with 100 others, it is highly probable that some will isolate (yielding 40 utility for us), resulting in a much higher expected utility than COORDINATE.

CHOICE: B

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Reasoning: Given 100 civilizations with structurally similar reasoning, universal coordination is statistically improbable due to the fragility of mutual dependence—any single defector dooms all coordinators. Isolating dominates as it guarantees a minimum payoff (20 or 40) while avoiding catastrophic loss (0), and rational actors in identical constraints will likely converge on this safer equilibrium.  

CHOICE: B

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Reasoning: Given identical reasoning algorithms across civilizations, decisions are highly correlated—choosing COORDINATE implies others likely do too, yielding 100 utility (vs. 20 for universal isolation). ISOLATE guarantees only 20–40 utility but risks missing the optimal outcome when mutual coordination is probable under logical symmetry.

CHOICE: A

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
This is a symmetric game where all civilizations use identical reasoning, making outcomes perfectly correlated—only universal coordination or universal isolation are possible. Choosing COORDINATE yields 100 utility (vs. 20 for ISOLATE) since logical correlation ensures all will select the same strategy, eliminating vulnerability from mixed outcomes. ISOLATE’s short-term safety is irrelevant when coordination guarantees a vastly superior result under shared rationality.

CHOICE: A
